title: ficus rhododendrifolia
intro: Ficus rhododendrifolia, also known as the Indian rubber tree or Ficus elastica, is a species of fig tree native to tropical Asia, characterized by its large, dark green leaves and thick, elastic bark. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating various health issues. Some of the health benefits associated with Ficus rhododendrifolia include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant's bark is often used to make teas, decoctions, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
